title: Severe backpressure on StreamOps pipeline
summary: Backpressure ratio reached 0.92 (threshold: 0.5) on the sink operator. Upstream operators are throttled, causing consumer lag to grow at 5,000 messages/minute. Processing latency rising as a secondary effect.
root_cause: Downstream sink (Kafka producer to stream-alerts topic) cannot keep up with the output rate. The alert topic may have insufficient partitions or the sink parallelism is too low.
timeline: T+0s: Backpressure scenario injected by simulator
T+5s: MetricGenerator begins emitting backpressure_ratio values in 0.8-1.0 range
T+20s: Agent detects backpressure via Flink job metrics
T+24s: Diagnostic Agent confirms cascading effect from sink to source
T+27s: Report Agent classifies as HIGH due to growing lag and SLA risk

recommended_actions.action: Increase sink operator parallelism from 1 to 4
recommended_actions.rationale: Sink is the bottleneck; increasing parallelism distributes the write load across more threads

recommended_actions.action: Add partitions to the stream-alerts topic
recommended_actions.rationale: Sink parallelism is bounded by partition count; more partitions enable more parallel writers

monitoring_notes: After scaling, monitor backpressure_ratio (should drop below 0.3) and consumer lag (should stabilize within 5 minutes). Also watch for checkpoint duration increases as state redistribution occurs.
